scenario: Aurora Bank queries the Camunda 8.8 Operate API to feed 4 dashboards that cover 9000 instances per day. The team writes its first POST /v1/process-instances/search request and needs to confirm the four documented top-level components of an Operate query request body, so pagination and sorting work exactly as the API expects across releases.
question: Which components make up a query request body for the Camunda 8.8 Operate API search endpoint?

options.text: A query consists of filter (object fields to match), size (max items to return), sort (field plus ASC or DESC), and searchAfter for pagination using sortValues from previous results
A query consists of where (object fields to match), limit (max items to return), orderBy (field plus ASC or DESC), and offset for pagination using a numeric row index from previous results
A query consists of must (object fields to match), top (max items to return), order (field plus ASC or DESC), and pageToken for pagination using an opaque token returned by previous results
A query consists of conditions (object fields to match), count (max items to return), rank (field plus ASC or DESC), and cursor for pagination using a base64 cursor returned by previous results

a.text: Correct. The Query section defines the request as filter (which fields to match), size (max items), sort (an array of {field, order} with order ASC or DESC), and searchAfter for pagination, where you copy the sortValues from the previous results into searchAfter to fetch the next page.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs use filter/size/sort/searchAfter, not SQL-style where/limit/orderBy/offset, and Operate paginates with search-after rather than numeric offsets.

c.text: Incorrect. The keys are filter/size/sort/searchAfter; there is no must/top/order/pageToken envelope in the documented query body.

d.text: Incorrect. There is no conditions/count/rank/cursor schema for Operate queries; the documented schema is filter/size/sort/searchAfter.

explanation: Operate API query bodies have four documented components: filter (which fields the items must match), size (maximum items to return, default 10), sort (array of {field, order} with order ASC or DESC, default sorts ascending by key), and searchAfter, which takes the sortValues array from the previous results to page forward.
